commented_code:
+name: Test
+
+on:
+  push:
+  pull_request:
+
+jobs:
+  test:
+    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
+    strategy:
+      matrix:
+        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]   
+        python-version: ['3.10'] 
+      fail-fast: false
+
+    steps:
+    - name: Checkout code
+      uses: actions/checkout@v5
+
+    - name: Set up Python
+      uses: actions/setup-python@v6
+      with:
+        python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
+
+    - name: Set up uv
+      uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v7
+    # Linux: Request 'cpu' extra to force the pytorch-cpu index (prevents libcublas crash)
+    - name: Install dependencies (Linux)
+      if: runner.os == 'Linux'
+      run: |
+        uv sync --extra cpu
+        uv pip install torch torchvision --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu
+
+    # macOS: Standard sync 
+    - name: Install dependencies (macOS)
+      if: runner.os == 'macOS'
+      run: uv sync
+
+    - name: Add nanochat to PYTHONPATH (Unix)
+      run: |
+        echo "PYTHONPATH=$(pwd):$PYTHONPATH" >> $GITHUB_ENV
+
+    - name: Run pytest
+      run: |
+        # uv run pytest tests/
+        .venv/bin/pytest tests/

discussion_comments.comment_body:
@karpathy: I personally think it would be nicer to be able to run `uv pip install -e .` here, instead of messing with env vars. But if we want that then we'd have to add

```
[tool.setuptools]
packages = ["nanochat"]
```

to `pyproject.toml`.

```suggestion
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - master
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - master
```

discussion_comments.comment_body:
can be removed, as `windows` isn't in the `os` matrix (anymore)
```suggestion
```

I don't think you want to run this for every branch? Typically, you'd do `master` or `main` only, and then any PR that gets opened (which you have in the next step). 
```suggestion
      - master
```

@svlandeg your first question is an answer to this. "What else is missing here that the tests aren't actually run yet on this PR? (maybe GH actions are currently disabled for this repo?)" I want to see it on my branch before pushing here. This is just to trigger easily. It can be changed while pushing by reviewers.
Ah gotcha, so you basically just set this so that your branch would test, but this should be reverted to `master` before merging this PR. Makes sense.
